snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review retains the published check-only Update Service contract and binds the compact Settings modal ownership repair on sole main c365e9e, including inert background ownership, the 720-to-420 focus transition and exact Escape focus restoration without changing layout, copy, persistence, service or API behavior.

reviewers.task: Update Service continuity and compact Settings modal ownership review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the compact Settings UI commit and independently compared its exact renderer and test bytes with the published Update Service baseline, Settings interaction contract and existing modal/focus owners on sole main c365e9e.

dimensions.summary: Settings remains the existing surface owner and the repair uses the established dialog, compact navigator and opener focus references; no duplicate modal, navigation or service owner is added.

The published Update Service contract remains unchanged, while compact and resident Settings now agree on one modal boundary: background chrome/workspace is inert, compact transition focus enters Settings, and Escape restores the exact opener.
Existing requirement, build, exit and phase statuses remain unchanged; the candidate adds focused interaction evidence only and does not promote Update Service or broader Settings completion.

Focused Settings, Home conversation and renderer tests cover inert ownership, resident-to-compact focus transfer, Tab containment and exact Escape restoration; typecheck, build and UI visual gates remain directly runnable.
The product delta remains confined to App.tsx and its focused UI regression; this excluded recipe records the independent publication review without adding or rewriting an owner document.

verification.command: Independent review of compact Settings modal ownership on sole main c365e9e
verification.evidence: Review confirms titlebar, sidebar and workspace become inert only while Settings is open; crossing into compact mode moves focus to the Settings navigator; Escape removes inert and restores the exact opener without renderer restyling or service changes.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused UI tests, typecheck, build and visual-contract verification bind the exact repair while the union recipe retains the published Reader, Update, release and security roots.

coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes only modal inert/focus ownership in the existing Settings renderer and its regression test; it adds no API, persistence, Job, permission, layout, locale or service behavior.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning reviewed the exact UI bytes on sole main c365e9e and records this bounded accessibility repair without restyling Settings or claiming broader feature completion.

nonBlockingRisks: The default adapter intentionally performs no network request; production signed-feed connectivity and its runtime dependency remain unselected and unimplemented.
No automatic schedule, download, install, restart, risky-job interlock or real packaged alpha-to-alpha update was produced; no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase status is promoted.
The existing real macOS 720-to-420 Settings evidence covers the foreground modal transition; this repair makes no claim about unrelated Windows titlebar geometry.
